title: aquilegia vulgaris var. nigricans
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris var. nigricans, also known as the black columbine, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides in its roots. The health benefits of the black columbine plant include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the roots in hot water or using the plant's extracts in the form of tinctures or salves, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
